content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms from autonomous ICM-equipped agents can be repurposed to automatically prioritize what new information gets encoded into long-term memory for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents, creating a self-tuning memory system that grows aligned with the agent's own task goals rather than human-designed retention rules. This replaces the common fixed-importance heuristic used in most modern active agent memory architectures with an autonomous, dynamically updating retention policy.
